methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Positron AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Monumo is a Series B semiconductors company founded by Dominic Vergine that has raised $100M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 8 private peers in chips and semiconductors at similar or adjacent stages (including Etched, d-Matrix, Positron AI, Substrate, and DEEPX) and 3 public semiconductor benchmarks (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-progression analysis uses the $100M total raised as a baseline and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $190M. Peer-median anchoring across 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables produces a $1.0B implied valuation, with the comp set ranging from $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp P/S anchoring cannot be applied because Monumo's revenue is not on file, which is standard for private companies. The public sector median of 24.4x P/S across the three tickers remains available as a calibration point if revenue becomes disclosed.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $162M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $595M. The wide dispersion reflects meaningful uncertainty around Monumo's relative position within the frontier-chip landscape—specifically, whether its technology profile and go-to-market position align more closely with earlier-stage peers or later-stage valuations like Positron AI's $1.0B Series B mark.
